conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with severely decreased glomerular filtration rate
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Reduced renal clearance in patients with CrCl <40 ml/min requires dose adjustment to prevent drug accumulation and potential adverse effects. Ramipril is minimally removed by hemodialysis, requiring careful dosing considerations in dialysis patients.
contraindications.riskDescription: Risk of drug accumulation and enhanced hypotensive effects due to decreased renal elimination
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Administer 25% of normal dose when CrCl <40 ml/min
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Ramipril: Administer 25% of normal dose when CrCl <40 ml/min. Minimally removed by hemodialysis.
